practical_recommendations.title: Word Order
practical_recommendations.guidance: Keep standard word order and avoid fragments or run-ons.
practical_recommendations.why: 2 cases (40% of all grammar issues). This makes ideas harder to follow on first read.

examples.error: I need one from one phrase
examples.correction: I need one phrase from you

practical_recommendations.title: Collocation
practical_recommendations.guidance: Use natural word combinations instead of literal translations.
practical_recommendations.why: 1 cases (20% of all grammar issues). This sounds translated and less native-like.

practical_recommendations.title: Articles
practical_recommendations.guidance: Use a/an/the (or zero article) correctly before nouns.
practical_recommendations.why: 1 cases (20% of all grammar issues). This reduces grammatical accuracy in noun phrases.

transcript:
Speaker A: Hi, I'm Roman, and there's a test speaking meeting. There was a first phrase and I need one from one phrase. One phrase from you.
Speaker B: Hi there, my name is Andrei, nice to meet you. Today.
Speaker A: Could you tell me what is cat shouting? Why you didn't kill him already?
Speaker B: He's a bit nervous because of vacuum cleaner. He just saw it on the floor in guest room and he decided that it's something strange for him and he's a bit scared, that's why he's meowing.
Speaker A: You need to pronounce correctly. Vacuum clear. Like at the back. Really? Okay.
Speaker B: That's it.

chunks.text:
Speaker A: Hi, I'm Roman, and there's a test speaking meeting. There was a first phrase and I need one from one phrase. One phrase from you.
Speaker B: Hi there, my name is Andrei, nice to meet you. Today.
Speaker A: Could you tell me what is cat shouting? Why you didn't kill him already?
Speaker B: He's a bit nervous because of vacuum cleaner. He just saw it on the floor in guest room and he decided that it's something strange for him and he's a bit scared, that's why he's meowing.
Speaker A: You need to pronounce correctly. Vacuum clear. Like at the back. Really? Okay.
Speaker B: That's it.

annotation_items.explanation: Unnatural word order/collocation—native English uses 'speaking test' (or just 'a test') rather than 'test speaking meeting'.

annotation_items.text: I need one from one phrase
annotation_items.correction: I need one phrase from you
annotation_items.explanation: Incorrect word order/structure; the intended meaning is 'I need one phrase from you' or 'I need one more phrase'.
Missing definite article before 'cat' when referring to a specific animal.

annotation_items.text: Why you didn't kill him already?
annotation_items.correction: Why didn't you kill him already?
annotation_items.explanation: Incorrect question word order — auxiliary verb should precede the subject.

annotation_items.text: on the floor in guest room
annotation_items.correction: on the floor in the guest room

annotation_items.text: he decided that it's something strange for him
annotation_items.explanation: Wrong verb choice ('decided' implies making a choice); use 'thought' or 'found' for perception; also adjust tense ('it's' → 'it was').
Incorrect or mispronounced word — the correct compound noun is 'vacuum cleaner'.

llm.annotated_transcript_html:
Speaker A: Hi, I'm Roman, and <mark class="grammar-error" title="COLLOC: Correct: there's a speaking test | Unnatural word order/collocation—native English uses 'speaking test' (or just 'a test') rather than 'test speaking meeting'.">there's a test speaking meeting</mark>. There was a first phrase and <mark class="grammar-error" title="ORDER: Correct: I need one phrase from you | Incorrect word order/structure; the intended meaning is 'I need one phrase from you' or 'I need one more phrase'.">I need one from one phrase</mark>. One phrase from you.
Speaker B: Hi there, my name is Andrei, nice to meet you. Today.
Speaker A: Could you tell me <mark class="grammar-error" title="ARTICLE: Correct: what is the cat shouting? | Missing definite article before 'cat' when referring to a specific animal.">what is cat shouting?</mark> <mark class="grammar-error" title="ORDER: Correct: Why didn't you kill him already? | Incorrect question word order — auxiliary verb should precede the subject.">Why you didn't kill him already?</mark>
Speaker B: He's a bit nervous <mark class="grammar-error" title="ARTICLE: Correct: because of the vacuum cleaner. | Missing definite article before 'vacuum cleaner'.">because of vacuum cleaner.</mark> He just saw it <mark class="grammar-error" title="ARTICLE: Correct: on the floor in the guest room | Missing definite article 'the' before 'guest room'.">on the floor in guest room</mark> and <mark class="grammar-error" title="WORD: Correct: he thought it was strange | Wrong verb choice ('decided' implies making a choice); use 'thought' or 'found' for perception; also adjust tense ('it's' → 'it was').">he decided that it's something strange for him</mark> and he's a bit scared, that's why he's meowing.
Speaker A: You need to pronounce correctly. <mark class="grammar-error" title="WORD: Correct: vacuum cleaner. | Incorrect or mispronounced word — the correct compound noun is 'vacuum cleaner'.">Vacuum clear.</mark> Like at the back. Really? Okay.
Speaker B: That's it.
